items.title: Bitwise calls HYPE ‘most mispriced’ crypto despite 77% rise this year
items.summary: Bitwise’s Matt Hougan says Hyperliquid’s token is not priced as if it is tied to a “global super-app,” arguing the platform is more than just a crypto platform.

items.title: Trump orders review of fintech firms' access to Fed payment services
items.summary: Financial regulators are also asked to review regulations that could be amended to streamline applications for eligible fintech firms seeking bank and credit union charters.

items.title: GitHub investigates unauthorized access to internal repositories
items.summary: GitHub said the activity involved the exfiltration of about 3,800 internal repositories, and it removed the malicious code extension.

items.title: Bitcoin, ether, XRP rebound as Senate curbs Trump's Iran war powers
items.summary: Bitcoin climbed to about $77,200, while XRP, ether and solana also gained as Treasury yields and oil fell.

items.title: Meta begins 8K global job cuts, starting in Singapore
items.summary: Approximately 49,000 workers were laid off in 2026 as companies adopted a more AI-reliant business model.

items.title: Non-dollar stablecoins are struggling to crack 0.5% of market share
items.summary: Everyone is building non-dollar stablecoins. But data shows that compared to USD-denominated stablecoins, almost no one is using them.

items.title: Bitcoin is falling, bond yields are rising. Yet BTC’s implied volatility, an uncertainty gauge, remains low.
items.summary: BTC's implied volatility remains low despite the recent price selloff. Options specialist prefers a long straddle strategy in this scenario.

items.title: US Senate advances resolution to curb Trump’s Iran war powers
items.summary: “Congress has the power to slam the brakes on this unwise conflict,” said bill sponsor and Democratic Senator Tim Kaine.

items.title: WLFI-linked AI Financial flags ‘going concern’ as losses mount
items.summary: AI Financial Corp. posted a net loss of $271.5 million in the first quarter of 2026 as it flagged significant doubt about its ability to continue operating for the next 12 months.

items.title: Bankr temporarily disables transactions after 14 wallets hacked
items.summary: Bankr recommends that affected users create a new wallet, generate a new seed phrase on a clean device and revoke approvals if remaining assets can’t be moved.

items.title: Trump-backed Truth Social pulls bids for crypto ETFs
items.summary: The crypto ETFs were intended to be part of Trump Media & Technology Group’s broader crypto strategy, which included the launch of the Truth.fi financial platform.

items.title: Solana futures funding rate turns negative: Is $78 SOL next?
items.summary: Solana futures funding turned negative as demand for SOL and its associated decentralized exchanges fell. Will traders buy the dip or is $78 next?

items.title: Trump orders government, Fed to review crypto firms' access to payment rails
items.summary: Donald Trump's executive order asks the Fed to review how depository institutions may be granted access to payment services, an area the crypto industry is deeply involved with.

items.title: CFTC sues Minnesota, Governor Tim Walz over prediction markets ban
items.summary: About 24 hours after Minnesota Governor Tim Walz signed a bill into law passed by the state’s legislature to effectively ban prediction markets in the state, the CFTC pushed back with its own lawsuit.

items.title: After Nearly a Decade, Gary Gensler's Poster Child for Crypto Compliance Executes Its First Trade
items.summary: After weathering years of industry skepticism and navigating a shifting regulatory landscape, Prometheum executed its first crypto trades.

items.title: Bernstein: Bitcoin miners becoming critical suppliers in AI infrastructure
items.summary: Bernstein says miners control 27 GW of planned power and $90 billion in AI deals, giving them a strategic edge as electricity becomes the main constraint on data center growth.

items.title: Minnesota Bans Prediction Markets—And Is Sued By the Trump Admin Hours Later
items.summary: Minnesota's ban has made it a felony to create or operate a prediction market in the state. The CFTC and DOJ say it violates federal law.

items.title: Even a mountain of T-bills won't save Tether and Circle from a sudden liquidity crisis, expert says
items.summary: The head of digital assets and tokenization at one of Germany’s largest asset managers said that USDT and USDC are not stablecoins, from his perspective.

items.title: Data shows Bitcoin dip buyers waiting for lower prices: Is $70K BTC’s next stop?
items.summary: Bitcoin futures and orderbook data show dip buyers waiting for a BTC price drop below $70,000.

items.title: This bitcoin bear market is different with 'uniquely pessimistic' traders limiting downside, K33 says
items.summary: The research firm said bitcoin traders remain unusually defensive, reducing the risk of the kind of leverage-driven collapse seen in prior downturns.

items.title: Americans Arrested in Japan for Entering Punch the Monkey’s Zoo Home to Promote Meme Coin
items.summary: Viral Japanese macaque monkey Punch received unwelcome visitors this week, as trespassers attempted to promote a Solana meme coin.

items.title: Bitcoin Miners Emerge as Unlikely Power Brokers in AI Infrastructure Race, Says Bernstein
items.summary: Bernstein remains bullish on Bitcoin mining firms like IREN, Riot, and CleanSpark, who are all riding the wave of AI compute demand.

items.title: Google Unveils Gemini Omni—A Next-Gen AI Video Builder That Can 'Simulate the World'
items.summary: Google's new multimodal AI model powers updates to Flow and Flow Music, including conversational video editing and AI-generated media tools.

items.title: Polymarket partners with Nasdaq to list private company contracts
items.summary: New markets will let users trade on fundraising, valuation and other startup milestones using data from Nasdaq Private Market, extending forecasting into private capital.

items.title: Google Launches Gemini Spark: A 24/7 AI Agent That Wants to Make You Ditch OpenClaw
items.summary: Announced at Google I/O 2026, Spark is Google's most ambitious Gemini feature yet—a personal agent that manages your inbox, your calendar, and your workflows, with or without you watching.

items.title: Zerohash pursues new funding at more than $1.5 billion valuation after Mastercard drops investment plans
items.summary: Crypto infrastructure providers are drawing renewed investor interest as Wall Street deepens its push into digital assets.

items.title: Hyperliquid’s HYPE one of crypto’s most undervalued assets, says Bitwise
items.summary: The crypto asset manager argued the market is mispricing Hyperliquid as a niche derivatives exchange instead of a fast-growing “super-app” for global trading markets.

items.title: Elizabeth Warren Calls Crypto Bank Charter Approvals for Firms Like Coinbase, Ripple Illegal
items.summary: In a letter to the Comptroller of the Currency, Warren argued nine national trust bank charter approvals for crypto firms violated the National Bank Act.

items.title: Senator Warren questions OCC chief on approval of ‘ineligible’ crypto trust charters
items.summary: The Massachusetts lawmaker asked the US Comptroller of the Currency to provide all communications between the agency and Donald Trump potentially related to the charter approvals.

items.title: The SEC wants to let newly public companies raise cash instantly in its biggest rule change in decades
items.summary: The agency is proposing its largest overhaul of public listing rules in over 20 years, cutting compliance costs and giving crypto firms a much easier path to raise cash on Wall Street.

items.title: BoE deputy says tokenization could cut costs, boost competition
items.summary: The UK central bank said digital money should remain trusted and interoperable as it weighs stablecoin reforms and near-24/7 settlement to support tokenized markets.

items.title: People Are More Willing to Lie to AI Than Humans, Study Finds
items.summary: Consumers feel less social pressure when lying to an AI chatbot, research finds, while human-like social cues reduce dishonest behavior.

items.title: The U.S. can’t lose the bitcoin race to China
items.summary: The next global power competition is not being fought over missiles alone. It’s being fought over money, and right now, China is moving aggressively to shape the future of it, argues Gooden.

items.title: XRP price risks 50% drop despite 9-day ETF inflow streak
items.summary: Spot XRP ETFs record net inflows for nine days, absorbing sell pressure and potentially supporting an XRP price recovery over time.
